subject: Computer Networking
title: DNSSEC: Trusting DNS Answers
description: A fast breakdown of DNSSEC: Trusting DNS Answers for builders.

hook.text: You ask DNS for a bank's IP. What if someone poisons the answer and sends you to a fake site?

buildup.text: DNSSEC adds digital signatures to DNS records. Your resolver can verify that the answer is authentic.

discovery.text: Signatures chain from the root zone down. Each level vouches for the next, building a chain of trust.

twist.text: DNSSEC doesn't encrypt queries — it only proves answers are genuine. Privacy needs DNS-over-HTTPS separately.

climax.text: Adoption is slow because it adds complexity and can break misconfigured domains. But it prevents cache poisoning.

punchline.text: DNSSEC proves the answer came from the right source.

quiz.question: What does DNSSEC protect against?
options: Forged DNS answers and cache poisoning
